title: P0: Navigate "go to nearest slime" moves to player instead of slime
description:
When user says "Go to nearest slime", the LLM returns navigate intent with null x/y/z (because the system prompt says to set coords to null for navigate). The navigate handler falls back to chat.PlayerPos — the PLAYER's position — instead of the nearest slime.

Root causes:
1. System prompt lumps ALL navigate intents under "set coords to null" rule
2. Entity summary in prompt only shows distance (slime@15blk), not positions — LLM can't output coordinates even if it wanted to
3. No server-side fallback: when navigate has null coords and item=entity type, should find nearest matching entity

Fixes needed:
- Update FormatEntitySummary to include positions
- Update system prompt navigate rules to allow entity-targeted coords
- Add entity-targeted navigation fallback in AgentBackgroundService

comments.author: anonymous
comments.body:
Fixed in Sprint 55 Wave C. Three changes applied:

1. **FormatEntitySummary** (AgentBackgroundService.cs ~line 2527): Entity summary now includes positions — `slime@15blk(90,4,120)` instead of just `slime@15blk`. This gives the LLM coordinates it can use.

2. **System prompt navigate rules** (LlmChatInterpreter.cs ~line 256): Updated to distinguish "come here" (null coords → player position) from "go to nearest <entity>" (look up entity coordinates from Nearby entities list in prompt).

3. **Entity-targeted navigation fallback** (AgentBackgroundService.cs ~line 1300): When navigate intent has null coords but `intent.Item` names an entity type (e.g., "slime"), the handler searches `nearbyEntitiesRaw` for the nearest matching entity and navigates to it. This is a server-side safety net in case the LLM still doesn't provide coords.

Files changed:
- `WebUI.Blazor/AgentBackgroundService.cs` — FormatEntitySummary + navigate fallback
- `Agent.Planning/LlmChatInterpreter.cs` — system prompt navigate rules
